checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet=3791594.01 equivalent quarter revenue: Revenues=3791594.01 (2012-10-01 to 2012-12-31)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?
Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Q filing_date 2013-11-12 one day before cutoff 2013-11-13, containing both 2012-12-31 comparative and Q3 2013 data

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent data series ends at 2011-09-30 with no later observations
DeferredRevenueCurrent data series ends at 2011-12-31 with no later observations despite continuing operations
Multiple overlapping/conflicting revenue tags (Revenues, SalesRevenueNet, SalesRevenueGoodsNet, SalesRevenueServicesNet) for overlapping periods with differing values

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction/elimination of the allowance for doubtful accounts while gross receivables were rapidly increasing, which understates bad debt expense and overstates net realizable receivables and reported income/equity.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 3475019.05 (2012-12-31) to 8246777.86 (2013-09-30), a ~137% increase in nine months

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Recognition of revenue drawn down from a large deferred revenue balance (contract liability) without matching cash inflow, producing multi-year positive net income alongside deeply negative operating cash flow; if recognition pace of the deferred balance was accelerated relative to performance obligations, revenue/income could be overstated in early periods.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=15980947.79 (2010 annual) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-40410794.39 (2010 annual); NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=18132439.97 (2011 annual) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-24882792.31 (2011 annual)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K income statement and cash flow statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature revenue recognition or extended customer credit terms (channel stuffing) in Q1 2013, where receivables grew far faster (+87% q/q) than revenue (+30% q/q), inflating reported sales relative to cash-collectible receivables.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=3475019.05 (2012-12-31) to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=6509268.34 (2013-03-31), an ~87% quarter-over-quarter increase
Revenues=3791594.01 (2012-10-01 to 2012-12-31) to SalesRevenueGoodsNet=4937351.87 (2013-01-01 to 2013-03-31), an ~30% quarter-over-quarter increase

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts zeroed out (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=0.0 at 2013-06-30 and 2013-09-30) while gross receivables grew ~137% in nine months (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent from 3,475,019.05 at 2012-12-31 to 8,246,777.86 at 2013-09-30)

Persistent 3-year divergence between positive net income and deeply negative operating cash flow (e.g., NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=18,132,439.97 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-24,882,792.31 in 2011)

Receivables growth (+87% q/q) sharply outpacing revenue growth (+30% q/q) in Q1 2013, coincident with a major business-model transition (deferred revenue base collapsing from ~$49.9M in 2010 to ~$4.1M in 2011)
Elevated 8-K filing frequency clustered in the weeks immediately preceding the cutoff date (five 8-Ks between 2013-09-25 and 2013-11-01)
Inconsistent/overlapping XBRL revenue tagging (Revenues, SalesRevenueNet, SalesRevenueGoodsNet, SalesRevenueServicesNet) across periods complicating trend verification
